# Deboa

> **Deboa** is a straightforward, non opinionated, developer-centric HTTP client library for Rust. 
It offers a rich array of modern features—from flexible authentication and serialization formats 
to runtime compatibility and middleware support—while maintaining simplicity and ease of use. 
It’s especially well-suited for Rust projects that require a lightweight, efficient HTTP client 
without sacrificing control or extensibility.

## Installation

```toml
[dependencies]
deboa = { version = "0.0.9", features = ["http1", "tokio-rt"] }
```

## Quickstart

```rust
use deboa::{Client, request::get, Result};

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Result> {
    let client = Client::new();
    
    // Make a GET request
    let response = get("https://httpbin.org/get")
        .send_with(&client)
        .await?;
        
    println!("Status: {}", response.status());
    println!("Body: {}", response.text().await?);
    
    Ok(())
}
```

## Features

- easily add, remove and update headers
- helpers to add basic and bearer auth
- set retries and timeout
- pluggable catchers (interceptors)
- pluggable compression (gzip, deflate, brotli)
- pluggable serialization (json, xml, msgpack, yaml, fory and cbor)
- cookies support
- urlencoded and multipart forms
- comprehensive error handling
- response streaming
- upgrade support (websocket, etc.)
- runtime compatibility (tokio and smol)
- http1/2 support 
- http3 support (planned)

## Crates

- [deboa](https://crates.io/crates/deboa)
- [deboa-tokio](https://crates.io/crates/deboa-tokio)
- [deboa-smol](https://crates.io/crates/deboa-smol)
- [deboa_macros](https://crates.io/crates/deboa_macros)
- [deboa_extras](https://crates.io/crates/deboa_extras)
- [vamo](https://crates.io/crates/vamo)
- [vamo_macros](https://crates.io/crates/vamo_macros)

## Examples

- [blog](https://github.com/ararog/deboa/blob/main/examples/blog): A simple blog application using Vamo. This example shows how to use
use Vamo to quickly make request to fetch posts from a REST api.
It also shows how to use DeboaCatcher trait to implement a simple middleware.
